title: After-Action Report — Times-Through-the-Order Familiarity & the Softball/Underhand Arsenal
scope: The through-line: O27's fiction needs un-timeable deception arms to be a *mathematically viable* counter to a bat-dominant, 12-batter, second-chance offense — not just flavor text in the README.

rationale.item: "Any sport has arbitrage — guys eventually learn how to crack the code. The idea that everyone employs a bunch of flamethrowers or corner painters to navigate the strike zone and is content to have 9+ ERAs is unrealistic. There would be players who figure out a way around it, and those are knuckleballers, junkballers, and people who adapt the mechanics of softball pitching onto O27 baseball."

There was no familiarity model. A pitcher faced the leadoff hitter for the 6th time in one 27-out arc with byte-identical effectiveness as the 1st. In O27 the top of the order gets 5–7 PAs against the same arm in a single half-inning, so familiarity should be the dominant pitching dynamic — and it was absent entirely.

A second, detailed brief asked for a batch of softball-mechanics pitches and rare "elite stopper" archetypes built around gravity manipulation, timing disruption, and fatigue immunity from a true underhand / ultra-low submarine slot.

The first pass shipped the seven pitches but flagged that the engine had no way to separate "grounder pitch" from "popup pitch" — both collapsed to weak contact. The designer's response: "then yes let's do that now. Feels like a good evolution in the game's modeling."

delegation_events.quote: "I do not want an offset tune. I don't care if runs scored are higher. I keep telling you this."
The designer's response: "then yes let's do that now. Feels like a good evolution in the game's modeling."

delegation_events.decision: Re-baseline the RE-curve heuristic 4× → 3.5× instead of tuning offense back down
delegation_events.quote: Since the higher-run environment is intentional and no offset tune is wanted, the heuristic was re-baselined 4× → 3.5× (with a comment), keeping it a guard against gross curve inversions while matching the new baseline.

delegation_events.decision: Do not add a redundant "underhand spinner" pitch
delegation_events.quote: The brief's "underhand spinner" eephus is mechanically identical to `peeled_drop` (topspin → weak grounders), so it was not added as a separate redundant entry.

actions.description: Times-through-the-order familiarity model: timing_resistance per-pitch attribute on all 24 catalog pitches, FAMILIARITY_* constants, matchup_pa tracking, familiarity threaded into _pitch_probs and contact_quality
Seven new softball/underhand catalog pitches (riseball, peeled_drop, backhand_changeup, sky_eephus, slither_knuck, drop_knuck, rise_knuck), 17 → 24 catalog
Three rare elite stopper archetypes (knuckleball_ace, softball_riseballer, underhand_eephus_artist) with new stamina_bonus archetype field
Pitch-mix bucket classifier updated for the new pitches; display-name overrides
launch_angle_bias per-pitch field redistributing ground_out ↔ fly_out (LAUNCH_REDIST_GO2FO = 0.35) plus synthetic launch-angle nudge; foul_delta per-pitch field added to the foul component
Glossary FB/BR/OFF% definitions extended; entries for deception, gb_pct, tto_hardhit_delta, tto_k_decay; decay reframed
Part 4 almanac stats: batted-ball profile (GB%/LD%/FB%/GO-AO), TTO hard-hit splits, Decay-vs-familiarity split, Deception grade, surfaced on player page

Part 5 engine-side TTO counters: {k,fo,bf}_tto buckets in SpellRecord/GameState, nine new game_pitcher_stats columns with idempotent migration, summed in sim, dedup view, almanac tto_k_decay, new invariant test

risk_posture: At `looks == 0` (first time facing this pitcher) `fam == 0` → every term collapses to the legacy probability surface. This preserves the realism identity invariant — `test_realism_identity` and all calibrated probabilities are untouched.
Identity at 0.0 (every pitch without the field). Sum-preserving, so total outs-vs-hits is unchanged — only the *type* of out shifts.
Per explicit instruction, no offsetting run-environment tune was applied.
Identity discipline: every lever added here collapses to the legacy surface at its neutral value — familiarity at `looks==0`, `launch_angle_bias`/`foul_delta` at `0.0`.
Exit velocity: unchanged by definition — `launch_angle_bias` is a launch-angle lever, not an EV one.

validation_claims.claim: python o27v2/manage.py smoke — 10/10 games complete (re-run after each part)
Stat-invariant gate (make test-invariants) against freshly-simmed DBs — 10/10 throughout; gate is now 11/11 with invariant 10
Familiarity table: flamethrower whiff% drops 10.1% → 7.0% by 6th look while knuckleballer only 10.1% → 9.3%
Grounder/flyout split table (8-team, 224-game sim): drop_knuck 25.3%/13.1%, rise_knuck 23.4%/17.0%, etc.
validation_claims.check_method: Re-sim 8-team pool and aggregate batted-ball type by pitch

validation_claims.claim: All 24 catalog pitches classified into exactly one pitch-mix bucket — verified none unbucketed and no typos
validation_claims.check_method: Cross-check catalog pitch names against the bucket classifier in o27v2/sim.py

validation_claims.claim: Verified by o27/almanac/tests/test_build.py (_deception_grade, _attach_pitcher_battedball_tto) and a full almanac build (694 pages)
validation_claims.check_method: pytest o27/almanac/tests/test_build.py and run an almanac build

validation_claims.claim: League K% by look over an 8-team / 150-game sim: 1st 26.4%, 2nd 24.0%, 3rd+ 18.6%; League TTO K-Decay = +7.8 K%-pts
validation_claims.check_method: Re-sim and aggregate the tto columns

validation_claims.claim: test_invariant_10_tto_buckets_reconcile asserts per-row bf_tto1+2+3 == batters_faced and k_tto1+2+3 == k
validation_claims.check_method: Read tests/test_stat_invariants.py and run the gate

validation_claims.claim: RE start:end ratio compressed from ≥4× to ~3.71× — confirmed on the 30-team default config, not an 8-team artifact
validation_claims.check_method: Re-derive RE curve from a 30-team sim

non_actions.reason: "I do not want an offset tune. I don't care if runs scored are higher. I keep telling you this."

non_actions.item: No per-pitch movement variance keyed to weather
non_actions.reason: The "knuckle-eephus catches every micro-current" idea has no hook; folded into the high-resistance / high-bb_delta knuckle variants rather than add a fake field
mechanically identical to peeled_drop (topspin → weak grounders), so it was not added as a separate redundant entry

non_actions.item: K%-by-look not derivable from game_pa_log in Part 4
non_actions.reason: game_pa_log stores balls-in-play but no K/BB rows; that dimension needs engine-side TTO counters

residual_risks: The magnitudes (FAMILIARITY_*, the per-pitch timing_resistance values) were set by reasoning, not a season-scale calibration sweep, so the size of the junkball edge over a full 162 is an estimate.
Rare pitches like riseball/peeled_drop/sky_eephus have tiny samples in an 8-team pool.
Per-pitcher TTO K-Decay is a small-sample split (the 3rd+ bucket is thin), meaningful in aggregate / over a full season.

Two pre-existing test anomalies confirmed independent of this branch: test_season_archive_writer_runs_end_to_end asserts wrc_plus in a category set that doesn't contain it; test_stat_invariants ERRORs with sqlite when the whole suite runs together (test-isolation issue).
open_hooks: FAMILIARITY_PER_LOOK is the single dial to scale the whole effect up or down.
K%-by-look engine counters were 'the clean follow-up' (delivered in Part 5 within this AAR).
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Times-Through-the-Order Familiarity & the Softball/Underhand Arsenal
Part 3 — Making the grounder/popup split a real outcome (launch_angle_bias + foul_delta)
Remaining modeling gaps (still deliberately not faked)
Part 4 — Stats surfaced from the new mechanics
Part 5 — Engine-side TTO counters: K%-by-look becomes real
unmapped_content: FAMILIARITY constants table: FAMILIARITY_PER_LOOK 0.18, FAMILIARITY_MAX_LOOKS 7, FAMILIARITY_SWINGING −0.022, FAMILIARITY_CALLED −0.009, FAMILIARITY_CONTACT +0.020, FAMILIARITY_HARD_TILT +0.028, DEFAULT_TIMING_RESISTANCE 0.5. Shape: fam = FAMILIARITY_PER_LOOK * min(looks, MAX) * factor; factor = clamp((1 - timing_resistance) * 2, 0, 2). timing_resistance examples: knuckleball 0.95, sky_eephus 0.92, eephus 0.85, four_seam 0.20. Keying matchup_pa on the pitcher means a fresh reliever resets familiarity — bringing in a new look is itself a manager lever. Pitch profile table for the seven new pitches and archetype details (weights 0.03/0.04/0.03, slots, stamina bonuses +0.18/+0.15, repertoire resistances ≈0.93/0.69/0.84). launch_angle_bias values per pitch (sinker −0.45, spitter −0.55, curve_10_to_2 −0.50, peeled_drop −0.70, drop_knuck −0.60, splitter −0.25; four_seam +0.30, eephus +0.20, sky_eephus +0.30, riseball +0.70, rise_knuck +0.50; ±8° at full bias). foul_delta values (riseball +0.04, sky_eephus +0.03, rise_knuck +0.03, eephus +0.02, four_seam +0.01). End-to-end wiring audit details (primary_pitch max-of-counts, arsenal chips, almanac reuses sim's percentages, no hardcoded enumerations in scout/valuation/awards/managers/engine_config/o27v2/archetypes.py). Commits: 6274a12, 7566137, 4891095, 5e2d61e; branch claude/sports-arbitrage-pitcher-mechanics-vVNgT. Part 4 stat definitions (GB <10°, LD 10–25°, FB >25°; Deception grade 20–80 scale; look = rank of ab_seq within (game,pitcher,batter)). Part 5 mechanics (BF bucket captured before matchup_pa tick; legacy rows default 0).
parser_notes: Five-part AAR with verbatim designer quotes; Parts 4 and 5 appended after the main verification section. Date from 'Date completed: 2026-05-27'.
